description: Displays all result layers generated from the Run AI Model tool.
Use the Eye icon to show or hide a layer on the map.
When a layer is displayed, the Analytics panel opens and shows a summary of the selected layer.
The Analytics panel allows you to view statistics by Area or Count.
Click the expand arrow beside a layer to view the prediction layers.
Click the three-dot menu to access additional layer options.

subdescription: Quickly navigate to the selected layer on the map.
To customize the appearance of the selected layer.
You can modify the fill color, stroke color, stroke width, and stroke opacity.
Preview the changes and click Save Style to apply them.
To divide the selected layer into equal-sized grid cells.
Set the required cell size using the '+' or '-' buttons and click Create Grid to generate the grid overlay.
To visualize areas with higher feature concentration.
Adjust the Blur and Radius sliders to refine the hotspot visualization for your analysis.
Export the selected layer for external use.
To permanently remove the selected layer from the current project.
Right-click anywhere on the map to add a geo-located comment and press Enter.
View all comments using the Comment in this section.
Click on any comment under the list to zoom into comment location on the map
Add reply by typing in the space under the respective comment box and press Enter.

description: Displays all assessment layers generated from the Assessment tool.
Use the Eye icon to show or hide the assessment layer on the map.
When an assessment layer is displayed, the Analytics panel opens and shows statistics for the selected assessment.
The Analytics panel allows you to view the assessment summary and statistics.
Click the three-dot menu to access additional layer options.

subdescription: Automatically adjusts the map view to the selected layer, allowing you to quickly locate and examine it.
To customize the appearance of the assessment layer.
Select the preferred classification method, define the number of classes, preview the changes, and click Save Style to apply them.
To divide the assessment layer into equal-sized grid cells.
Adjust the cell size using the '+' or '-' buttons and click Create Grid to generate the grid overlay.
To permanently remove the selected layer from the current project.
Right-click anywhere on the map to add a geo-located comment and press Enter.
View all comments using the Comment in this section.
Click on any comment under the list to zoom into comment location on the map
Add reply by typing in the space under the respective comment box and press Enter.

description: Displays all vector layers added using the Pull Vector tool.
Use the Eye icon to show or hide the vector layer on the map.
Click the expand arrow beside a layer to view the available vector layers.
Click the three-dot menu to access additional layer options.

subdescription: Automatically adjusts the map view to the selected layer, allowing you to quickly locate and examine it.
To customize the appearance of the selected vector layer.
You can modify the fill colour, fill opacity, border colour, border width, and border opacity.
Preview the changes and click Save Style to apply them.
To permanently remove the selected layer from the current project.
Right-click anywhere on the map to add a geo-located comment and press Enter.
View all comments using the Comment in this section.
Click on any comment under the list to zoom into comment location on the map
Add reply by typing in the space under the respective comment box and press Enter.

description: This section displays all annotation layers created using the Annotate tool.
Use the Eye icon to show or hide an annotation layer on the map.
